LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMEN CLINICS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT MEETS ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY DEMONSTRATING THAT DR. ELWYN WAS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. (WATKINS DEC., PARS. 2-3). IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT IS LIABLE BASED UPON PRINCIPLES OF OSTENSIBLE AUTHORITY. DR. ELWYN USES DEFENDANT'S LOGO AND WEBSITE. DEFENDANT HELD DR. ELWYN OUT AS ITS AGENT/EMPLOYEE. SEE CC 2300; 2317; YANCHOR V. KAGAN (1971) 22 CAL.APP.3D 544, 550. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH MAY INCLUDE LANGUAGE REGARDING THE INDICIA OF THE BILLING RECORDS ADDRESSED BY THE COURT AT HEARING. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Amen Clinics, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. PAUL J. STEINER, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Paul J. Steiner, Tel No. 415-981-6100 appearing for Plaintiff Sam Trinh, Guardian ad Litem for Christopher Trinh;
Atty. KATHLEEN A. HUMPHREY, ESQ. of Supple & Canvel, LLP, Tel No. 415-366-5533 appearing for Defendant Amen Clinics, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMEN CLINICS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant meets its burden of production by demonstrating that Dr. Elwyn was an independent contractor. (Watkins Dec., pars. 2-3). In opposition, plaintiff creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant is liable based upon principles of ostensible authority. Dr. Elwyn uses defendant's logo and website. Defendant held Dr. Elwyn out as its agent/employee. See CC 2300; 2317; Yanchor v. Kagan (1971) 22 Cal.App.3d 544, 550. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order which may include language regarding the indicia of the billing records addressed by the Court at hearing. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has maintained its summary judgment burden of production based on the declaration of Dr. Nickles. He states that Dr. Wong complied with the standard of care. Plaintiff must then present competent medical expert testimony to avoid summary judgment. See Munro v. UC Regents, (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985 ("When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence."). The court admits the declaration of Dr. Dusay and denies summary judgment. At par. 10 of his declaration, Dr. Dusay explains that based on Mr. Trinh's depression and anxiety, which Kaiser Permanente was aware of, and the warnings on the medication, Dr. Wong breached the standard of care. Dr. Wong was not merely acting as a pharmacist; he was treating Mr. Trinh. Given the conflicting expert opinions, a triable issue of material fact is presented as to breach of the standard of care. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2015 Continued To Mar-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to March 26, 2015 per agreement of parties. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
